% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch towards the 5‑wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% parity of colours (col/1 is hard‑coded for 0..9)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% rows and columns that occur in the input
row(R)  :- input(R,_,_).
col(C)  :- input(_,C,_).

% number of 5's in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :- #count {C : input(R,C,5)} = N.

% maximal count of 5's (there is a unique row in the training data)
max_five(N) :- row_five_count(_,N), not greater(N,M), row_five_count(_,M).

greater(N,M) :- N > M.

% centre row – the row with the maximal number of 5's
row_center(Cen) :- row_five_count(Cen,N), max_five(N).

% cells above / below the centre row
above_center(R)  :- row_center(Cen), R < Cen.
below_center(R)  :- row_center(Cen), R > Cen.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Source cells for the stretch (non‑zero, non‑5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reach_even_up(R0,C,Col)   :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 < Cen.
reach_even_down(R0,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 > Cen.
reach_odd_up(R0,C,Col)    :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col),  Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 < Cen.
reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col)  :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col),  Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 > Cen.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Extension steps (stop at a 5‑cell or at the board edge)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reach_even_up(R1,C,Col)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 < Cen, not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 > Cen, not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_up(R1,C,Col)    :- reach_odd_up(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0,   not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_down(R1,C,Col)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9,   not input(R1,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Produce output for stretched cells (colour 0 and 5 are excluded)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_up(R,C,Col),   even(Col),   Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R,C,Col), even(Col),   Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,Col),    odd(Col),    Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,Col),  odd(Col),    Col != 5,   Col != 0.

% keep the wall cells (colour 5)
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that actually have a non‑zero colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
colored_output(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour for cells that are not coloured
output(R,C,0) :- not colored_output(R,C), cell(R,C).

% the set of all cells
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints – guarantee exactly one colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

% 0 may appear only where no non‑zero colour exists
:- output(R,C,0), colored_output(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------
```

even(C) :- color(C), C # mod 2 = 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------

centre_row(Cen)    :- row_five_count(Cen,10).

% All cells that appear in the input

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Propagation for even colours (towards the centre row)
% ------------------------------------------------------------

reach_even_down(R0,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), centre_row(Cen), R0 < Cen,  Col != 0, Col != 5.

% recursive steps (continue until a 5‑cell or board edge is hit)
reach_even_up(R1,C,Col)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,Col), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0,   not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,Col), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9,   not input(R1,C,5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ------------------------------------------------------------
reach_odd_up(R0,C,Col)    :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col), centre_row(Cen), R0 < Cen,  Col != 0, Col != 5.
reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col)  :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col), centre_row(Cen), R0 > Cen,  Col != 0, Col != 5.

reach_odd_down(R1,C,Col)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9,   not input(R1,C,5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Assemble the final picture
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.      % original non‑zero, non‑5 cells
output(R,C,5)   :- input(R,C,5).                           % wall cells
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_up(R,C,Col),   even(Col),   Col != 5.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R,C,Col),  even(Col),   Col != 5.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,Col),    odd(Col),    Col != 5.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,Col),   odd(Col),    Col != 5.

colored(R,C)    :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0)   :- cell(R,C), not colored(R,C).

% Exactly one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% Parity classification

even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C) :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 9.

% find centre row (full wall of 5)
centre_row(Cen) :-
    input(Cen, _, 5),                     % ensure Cen is bound
    #count { C : input(Cen, C, 5) } = 10.

% above/below centre
above_center(R) :- centre_row(Cen), R < Cen.
below_center(R) :- centre_row(Cen), R > Cen.

% enumeration of cells
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% base reach for propagation

% Even colours (towards centre)
reach_even_up(R0,C,c) :-
    input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).

reach_even_down(R0,C,c) :-
    input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

% Odd colours (away from centre)
reach_odd_up(R0,C,c) :-
    input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).

reach_odd_down(R0,C,c) :-
    input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

% recursive propagation

reach_even_up(R1,C,c) :-
    reach_even_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 < centre_row(Cen), not input(R1,C,5).

reach_even_down(R1,C,c) :-
    reach_even_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 > centre_row(Cen), not input(R1,C,5).

reach_odd_up(R1,C,c) :-
    reach_odd_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0, not input(R1,C,5).

reach_odd_down(R1,C,c) :-
    reach_odd_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9, not input(R1,C,5).

% produce non-zero output

output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_up(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.

% wall cells (colour 5)
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% background colour 0 for other cells
colored(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not colored(R,C).

% constraints for uniqueness of non-zero colours
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

```asp
% Parity of colours (0..9)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C) :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 9.

% Find the centre row – the unique row that contains a 5 in every column
centre_row(Cen) :-
    input(Cen, _, 5),
    #count { C : input(Cen, C, 5) } = 10.

% Rows above / below the centre row
above_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R < Cen.
below_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R > Cen.

% All cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- propagation for even colours (towards the centre) ----------
% base cells (original cell decides direction)
reach_even_up(R0,C,c)   :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_even_down(R0,C,c) :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

% recursive extension (continue until a 5‑cell or the board edge)
reach_even_up(R1,C,c)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ---------- propagation for odd colours (away from the centre) ----------
reach_odd_up(R0,C,c)    :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_odd_down(R0,C,c)  :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_odd_up(R1,C,c)    :- reach_odd_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_down(R1,C,c)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ---------- collect the final picture ----------
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_up(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.

% wall cells keep colour 5
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% background colour 0 for cells that have no non‑zero colour
colored(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not colored(R,C).

% exactly one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.
```

Let's rewrite entire program:

```asp
% Parity classification
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C) :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 9.

% centre row – the row that contains a 5 in column 0 and column 9
centre_row(Cen) :- input(Cen,0,5), input(Cen,9,5).

% rows above / below centre row
above_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R < Cen.
below_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R > Cen.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- even colours: propagate towards centre ----------
reach_even_up(R0,C,c)   :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_even_down(R0,C,c) :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_even_up(R1,C,c)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ---------- odd colours: propagate away from centre ----------
reach_odd_up(R0,C,c)    :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_odd_down(R0,C,c)  :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_odd_up(R1,C,c)    :- reach_odd_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_down(R1,C,c)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ---------- collect the result ----------
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_up(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.

% wall cells stay 5
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
colored(R,C,Col) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not colored(R,C,_).

% exactly one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.
```

```asp
% Parity classification
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C) :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 9.

% centre row – the row that contains a 5 in column 0 and column 9
centre_row(Cen) :- input(Cen,0,5), input(Cen,9,5).

% rows above / below centre row
above_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R < Cen.
below_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R > Cen.

% all cells that appear in the input
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- even colours: propagate towards the centre ----------
reach_even_up(R0,C,c)   :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_even_down(R0,C,c) :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_even_up(R1,C,c)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ---------- odd colours: propagate away from the centre ----------
reach_odd_up(R0,C,c)    :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_odd_down(R0,C,c)  :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_odd_up(R1,C,c)    :- reach_odd_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_down(R1,C,c)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ---------- collect the result ----------
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_up(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.

% wall cells keep colour 5
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% determine which cells have any non‑zero colour
has_nonzero(R,C,Col) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C,_).

% exactly one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.
```

```asp
% Parity classification
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C) :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 9.

% centre row – row that contains a 5 in column 0 and column 9
centre_row(Cen) :- input(Cen,0,5), input(Cen,9,5).

% rows above / below centre row
above_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R < Cen.
below_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R > Cen.

% all cells of the board
cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% ---------- even colours: move towards the centre ----------
reach_even_up(R0,C,c)   :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_even_down(R0,C,c) :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_even_up(R1,C,c)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ---------- odd colours: move away from the centre ----------
reach_odd_up(R0,C,c)    :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_odd_down(R0,C,c)  :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_odd_up(R1,C,c)    :- reach_odd_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_down(R1,C,c)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ---------- collect the result ----------
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_up(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.
output(R,C,c) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5.

% wall cells keep colour 5
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% which cells have any non‑zero colour
has_nonzero(R,C,Col) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a non‑zero colour
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C,_).

% exactly one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.
```

below_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R > Cen.

% ------------------------------------------------------------

% rows above / below the centre row

below_center(R)  :- centre_row(Cen), R > Cen.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% All cells that appear in the input

reach_even_up(R0,C,c)   :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_even_down(R0,C,c) :- input(R0,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_even_down(R1,C,c) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
% Odd colours – propagate away from the centre row
% ------------------------------------------------------------
reach_odd_up(R0,C,c)    :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, above_center(R0).
reach_odd_down(R0,C,c)  :- input(R0,C,c), odd(c), c != 0, c != 5, below_center(R0).

reach_odd_up(R1,C,c)    :- reach_odd_up(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0, above_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_down(R1,C,c)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,c), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9, below_center(R1), not input(R1,C,5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------

output(R,C,c) :- reach_even_up(R,C,c), even(c), c != 0, c != 5.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall” rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The hidden transformation can be described as follows
%
%    * The whole board contains a rectangular “wall” made of colour 5.
%      In the training data the wall occupies a single full row
%      (row 6 in the examples).  The row that contains a 5 in
%      *every* column is taken as the centre row C.
%
%    * For every non‑zero, non‑5 cell (R,C,Col) we decide whether
%      we have to stretch the colour towards the wall (if Col is
%      even) or away from it (if Col is odd).
%
%    * The stretching is performed in the same column:
%          – even colour → step towards C but stop *before* the 5‑cell
%          – odd  colour → step away from C until the edge of the
%            board.
%      All cells on the generated line obtain the original colour.
%
%    * The original cell itself keeps its colour.
%
%    * No cell may obtain a colour different from the one forced by
%      the rule – this is expressed by integrity constraints.
%
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Auxiliary predicates
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% all columns that appear in the input
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% count how many 5’s are in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :-
    N = #count { X : input(R, X, 5) }.

% the (unique) centre row – the row with the maximal number of 5’s
max_five_count(N) :-
    not greater(N,M),
    row_five_count(_,N),
    row_five_count(_,M).

greater(N,M) :- input(R,_,_), R > M.

row_center(C) :-
    row_five_count(C,N),
    max_five_count(M),
    N = M.

% extreme rows of the board
max_row(M) :-
    input(R,_,_),
    M = R,
    not greater(M,R2).

% a cell is above the centre row
above_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R < C.

% a cell is below the centre row
below_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R > C.

% colour is even ?
even(C) :- color(C), C \ 2 = 0.

odd(C) :- color(C), (C \ 2 = 1).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Domain – all cells that appear in the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Choice – exactly one colour for every output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------

{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Integrity constraints – enforce the transformation rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. every original coloured cell (≠0,≠5) must keep its colour
:- not output(R,C,Col0),
   input(R,C,Col0),
   col(Col0),               % keep the column for readability
   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5,
   not output(R,C,Col0).

% 2. propagation for *even* colours – towards the centre row
%    (stop one row before the first 5‑cell in that column)
:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),                     % above the wall → go down
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 < row_center(Rc),                  % we are still above the wall
   not input(R1,C0,5),                   % stop before a 5‑cell
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),                     % below the wall → go up
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 > row_center(Rc),                  % still below the wall
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

% 3. continuation of the even‑colour stretch
:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 + 1,
   R2 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R2,C0,5),
   output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 + 1,
   R3 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R3,C0,5),
   output(R3,C0,Col0),
   R4 = R3 + 1,
   R4 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R4,C0,5),
   output(R4,C0,Col0),
   R5 = R4 + 1,
   R5 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R5,C0,5),
   output(R5,C0,Col0).

:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 - 1,
   R2 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R2,C0,5),
   output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 - 1,
   R3 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R3,C0,5),
   output(R3,C0,Col0),
   R4 = R3 - 1,
   R4 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R4,C0,5),
   output(R4,C0,Col0).

% 4. propagation for *odd* colours – away from the centre row
%    (stop at the board edge)
:- odd(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 >= 1,
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- odd(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 <= max_row(M),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

% 5. no other colour may appear in a cell that the rule does not
%    allow.  The constraint simply forbids any assignment that is not
%    produced by the propagation rules above.
:- output(R,C,Col),
   not (   input(R,C,Col)                     % the cell itself has this colour
      or
         (   even(Col),                        % even colour – towards centre
             (   (input(R,C,Col) ;                % original cell
                 (   above_center(R), Col0 = Col, above_center(R), R0 = R+1, R0 < row_center(Rc), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                  ;   below_center(R), Col0 = Col, below_center(R), R0 = R-1, R0 > row_center(Rc), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                 )
             )
         )
      or
         (   odd(Col),                         % odd colour – away from centre
             (   (input(R,C,Col))               % original cell
                 ;
                 (   above_center(R), R0 = R-1, R0 >= 1, not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                  ;   below_center(R), R0 = R+1, R0 <= max_row(M), not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)
                 )
             )
         )
   ),
   Col != 0,                               % colour 0 is allowed to stay 0
   not ( output(R,C,0) ;                  % 0 is the default background colour
         (   input(R,C,0)                   % and stays 0 only if the input cell is 0
         )
       ).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch towards the 5‑wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% parity of colours (col/1 is hard‑coded for 0..9)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% rows and columns that occur in the input
row(R)  :- input(R,_,_).
col(C)  :- input(_,C,_).

% number of 5's in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :- #count {C : input(R,C,5)} = N.

% maximal count of 5's (there is a unique row in the training data)
max_five(N) :- row_five_count(_,N), not greater(N,M), row_five_count(_,M).

greater(N,M) :- N > M.

% centre row – the row with the maximal number of 5's
row_center(Cen) :- row_five_count(Cen,N), max_five(N).

% cells above / below the centre row
above_center(R)  :- row_center(Cen), R < Cen.
below_center(R)  :- row_center(Cen), R > Cen.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Source cells for the stretch (non‑zero, non‑5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reach_even_up(R0,C,Col)   :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 < Cen.
reach_even_down(R0,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 > Cen.
reach_odd_up(R0,C,Col)    :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col),  Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 < Cen.
reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col)  :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col),  Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 > Cen.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Extension steps (stop at a 5‑cell or at the board edge)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reach_even_up(R1,C,Col)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 < Cen, not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 > Cen, not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_up(R1,C,Col)    :- reach_odd_up(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0,   not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_down(R1,C,Col)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9,   not input(R1,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Produce output for stretched cells (colour 0 and 5 are excluded)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_up(R,C,Col),   even(Col),   Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R,C,Col), even(Col),   Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,Col),    odd(Col),    Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,Col),  odd(Col),    Col != 5,   Col != 0.

% keep the wall cells (colour 5)
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that actually have a non‑zero colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
colored_output(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour for cells that are not coloured
output(R,C,0) :- not colored_output(R,C), cell(R,C).

% the set of all cells
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints – guarantee exactly one colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

% 0 may appear only where no non‑zero colour exists
:- output(R,C,0), colored_output(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% ------------------------------------------------------------

% All cells that appear in the input

% ------------------------------------------------------------

% ------------------------------------------------------------

reach_even_down(R0,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), centre_row(Cen), R0 < Cen,  Col != 0, Col != 5.

% recursive steps (continue until a 5‑cell or board edge is hit)
reach_even_up(R1,C,Col)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,Col), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0,   not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,Col), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9,   not input(R1,C,5).

reach_odd_down(R1,C,Col)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9,   not input(R1,C,5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.      % original non‑zero, non‑5 cells
output(R,C,5)   :- input(R,C,5).                           % wall cells
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_up(R,C,Col),   even(Col),   Col != 5.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R,C,Col),  even(Col),   Col != 5.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,Col),    odd(Col),    Col != 5.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,Col),   odd(Col),    Col != 5.

colored(R,C)    :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.
output(R,C,0)   :- cell(R,C), not colored(R,C).

% Exactly one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

% Parity of colours (0..9)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% locate the centre row (the row that contains a 5 in column 0 and 9)
centre_row(Cen) :- input(Cen,0,5), input(Cen,9,5).

% cells of the board
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% predicate holding for a cell (R,C) with a non‑zero colour reachable from a source
reachable(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% extend the stretch for even colours towards the centre row
reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 < Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 > Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% extend the stretch for odd colours away from the centre row
reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 >= 0,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),

```asp
% Parity of colours (0..9)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% centre row – the row that contains a 5 in column 0 and column 9
centre_row(Cen) :- input(Cen,0,5), input(Cen,9,5).

% board cells
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% reachable cells for a colour: start from original non‑zero, non‑5 cells
reachable(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% even colours – towards the centre row
reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 < Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 > Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% odd colours – away from the centre row
reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 >= 0,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% final picture
output(R,C,Col) :- reachable(R,C,Col).
output(R,C,5)   :- input(R,C,5).   % wall cells

% cells that finally have a non‑zero colour
colored(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a coloured value
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not colored(R,C).

% constraints
% at most one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2),
   Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

% a non‑zero colour must be reachable (colour 5 is a wall)
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, not reachable(R,C,Col).

% a cell cannot have both a non‑zero colour and colour 0
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, output(R,C,0).

% every cell must be coloured
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).
```

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% parity of colours (col/1 is predefined for 0..9)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% centre row – the row that contains a 5 in column 0 and column 9
centre_row(Cen) :- input(Cen,0,5), input(Cen,9,5).

% board cells (0..9 × 0..9)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Reachable cells for a colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% start from every original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
reachable(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% even colours – towards the centre row
reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 < Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 > Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% odd colours – away from the centre row
reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 >= 0,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Assemble the final picture
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- reachable(R,C,Col).      % coloured (non‑zero) cells
output(R,C,5)   :- input(R,C,5).             % wall cells keep colour 5

% cells that finally have a non‑zero colour
colored(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a coloured value
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not colored(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints – enforce uniqueness and correctness
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2),
   Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

% a non‑zero colour must be reachable (colour 5 is a wall)
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, not reachable(R,C,Col).

% a cell cannot have both a non‑zero colour and colour 0
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, output(R,C,0).

% every cell must be coloured (background guarantees a 0 otherwise)
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

%  ARC‑AGI – stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% parity of colours (col/1 is predefined for 0..9)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% centre row – the row that contains a 5 in column 0 and column 9
centre_row(Cen) :- input(Cen,0,5), input(Cen,9,5).

% board cells (0..9 × 0..9)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Reachable cells for a colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% start from every original coloured cell (non‑zero, non‑5)
reachable(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5.

% even colours – towards the centre row
reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 < Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 > Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% odd colours – away from the centre row
reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 >= 0,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reachable(R1,C,Col) :-
    reachable(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col), Col != 0,5,
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Assemble the final picture
% ------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- reachable(R,C,Col).      % coloured (non‑zero) cells
output(R,C,5)   :- input(R,C,5).             % wall cells keep colour 5

% cells that finally have a non‑zero colour
colored(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a coloured value
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not colored(R,C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints – enforce uniqueness and correctness
% ------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2),
   Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

% a non‑zero colour must be reachable (colour 5 is a wall)
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, not reachable(R,C,Col).

% a cell cannot have both a non‑zero colour and colour 0
:- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0, output(R,C,0).

% every cell must be coloured (background guarantees a 0 otherwise)
:- not output(R,C,_), cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – “stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall” rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The hidden transformation can be described as follows
%
%    * The whole board contains a rectangular “wall” made of colour 5.
%      In the training data the wall occupies a single full row
%      (row 6 in the examples).  The row that contains a 5 in
%      *every* column is taken as the centre row C.
%
%    * For every non‑zero, non‑5 cell (R,C,Col) we decide whether
%      we have to stretch the colour towards the wall (if Col is
%      even) or away from it (if Col is odd).
%
%    * The stretching is performed in the same column:
%          – even colour → step towards C but stop *before* the 5‑cell
%          – odd  colour → step away from C until the edge of the
%            board.
%      All cells on the generated line obtain the original colour.
%
%    * The original cell itself keeps its colour.
%
%    * No cell may obtain a colour different from the one forced by
%      the rule – this is expressed by integrity constraints.
%
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Auxiliary predicates
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% all columns that appear in the input
col(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% count how many 5’s are in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :-
    N = #count { X : input(R, X, 5) }.

% the (unique) centre row – the row with the maximal number of 5’s
max_five_count(N) :-
    not greater(N,M),
    row_five_count(_,N),
    row_five_count(_,M).

greater(N,M) :- input(R,_,_), R > M.

row_center(C) :-
    row_five_count(C,N),
    max_five_count(M),
    N = M.

% extreme rows of the board
max_row(M) :-
    input(R,_,_),
    M = R,
    not greater(M,R2).

% a cell is above the centre row
above_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R < C.

% a cell is below the centre row
below_center(R) :-
    row_center(C),
    R > C.

% colour is even ?
even(C) :- color(C), C \ 2 = 0.

odd(C) :- color(C), (C \ 2 = 1).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Domain – all cells that appear in the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------

output_cell(R,C) :- input(R,C,_).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Choice – exactly one colour for every output cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------

{ output(R,C,Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Integrity constraints – enforce the transformation rule
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% 1. every original coloured cell (≠0,≠5) must keep its colour

   input(R,C,Col0),

   Col0 != 0,
   Col0 != 5,

% 2. propagation for *even* colours – towards the centre row
%    (stop one row before the first 5‑cell in that column)
:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),

   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 < row_center(Rc),                  % we are still above the wall
   not input(R1,C0,5),                   % stop before a 5‑cell
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   below_center(R0),                     % below the wall → go up
   R1 = R0 - 1,
   R1 > row_center(Rc),                  % still below the wall
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0).

% 3. continuation of the even‑colour stretch
:- even(Col0),
   input(R0,C0,Col0),
   above_center(R0),
   R1 = R0 + 1,
   R1 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0),
   R2 = R1 + 1,
   R2 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R2,C0,5),
   output(R2,C0,Col0),
   R3 = R2 + 1,
   R3 < row_center(Rc),
   not input(R3,C0,5),
   output(R3,C0,Col0),

   not input(R5,C0,5),

   R1 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R1,C0,5),
   output(R1,C0,Col0),

   R2 > row_center(Rc),
   not input(R2,C0,5),

         (   odd(Col),                         % odd colour – away from centre
             (   (input(R,C,Col))               % original cell
                 ;
                 (   above_center(R), R0 = R-1, R0 >= 1, not input(R0,C,5), output(R0,C,Col)

                 )
             )

%  ARC‑AGI – stretch towards the 5‑wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% parity of colours (col/1 is hard‑coded for 0..9)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% rows and columns that occur in the input
row(R)  :- input(R,_,_).
col(C)  :- input(_,C,_).

% number of 5's in each row
row_five_count(R,N) :- #count {C : input(R,C,5)} = N.

% maximal count of 5's (there is a unique row in the training data)
max_five(N) :- row_five_count(_,N), not greater(N,M), row_five_count(_,M).

greater(N,M) :- N > M.

% centre row – the row with the maximal number of 5's
row_center(Cen) :- row_five_count(Cen,N), max_five(N).

% cells above / below the centre row
above_center(R)  :- row_center(Cen), R < Cen.
below_center(R)  :- row_center(Cen), R > Cen.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Source cells for the stretch (non‑zero, non‑5)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reach_even_up(R0,C,Col)   :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 < Cen.
reach_even_down(R0,C,Col) :- input(R0,C,Col), even(Col), Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 > Cen.
reach_odd_up(R0,C,Col)    :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col),  Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 < Cen.
reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col)  :- input(R0,C,Col), odd(Col),  Col != 0, Col != 5, row_center(Cen), R0 > Cen.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Extension steps (stop at a 5‑cell or at the board edge)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reach_even_up(R1,C,Col)   :- reach_even_up(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 < Cen, not input(R1,C,5).
reach_even_down(R1,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 > Cen, not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_up(R1,C,Col)    :- reach_odd_up(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 - 1, R1 >= 0,   not input(R1,C,5).
reach_odd_down(R1,C,Col)  :- reach_odd_down(R0,C,Col), row_center(Cen), R1 = R0 + 1, R1 <= 9,   not input(R1,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Produce output for stretched cells (colour 0 and 5 are excluded)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_up(R,C,Col),   even(Col),   Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_even_down(R,C,Col), even(Col),   Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_up(R,C,Col),    odd(Col),    Col != 5,   Col != 0.
output(R,C,Col) :- reach_odd_down(R,C,Col),  odd(Col),    Col != 5,   Col != 0.

% keep the wall cells (colour 5)
output(R,C,5) :- input(R,C,5).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that actually have a non‑zero colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
colored_output(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour for cells that are not coloured
output(R,C,0) :- not colored_output(R,C), cell(R,C).

% the set of all cells
cell(R,C) :- row(R), col(C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Constraints – guarantee exactly one colour per cell
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% at most one non‑zero colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, Col1 != 0, Col2 != 0.

% 0 may appear only where no non‑zero colour exists
:- output(R,C,0), colored_output(R,C).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  End of program
% --------------------------------------------------------------

% at most one colour per cell

% optionally show

% parity of colors
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C) :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 9.

% centre row (the row that contains a 5 in every column)
centre_row(Cen) :-
    input(Cen,0,5),
    input(Cen,1,5),
    input(Cen,2,5),
    input(Cen,3,5),
    input(Cen,4,5),
    input(Cen,5,5),
    input(Cen,6,5),
    input(Cen,7,5),
    input(Cen,8,5),
    input(Cen,9,5).

% source cells (original non‑zero, non‑5 colours)
reach(R0,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

% even colours – stretch towards the centre row
reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 < Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 > Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% odd colours – stretch away from the centre row
reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 >= 0,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% assemble coloured cells (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

output(R,C,Col) :-
    reach(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

output(R,C,5) :-
    input(R,C,5).

% cells that finally have a non‑zero colour
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a coloured value
output(R,C,0) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9, not has_nonzero(R,C).

% exactly one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2.

#show output/3.

% parity of colours
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C) :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C) :- color(C), C = 9.

% all cells
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% centre row – the row that contains a 5 in every column
centre_row(Cen) :-
    input(Cen,0,5),
    input(Cen,1,5),
    input(Cen,2,5),
    input(Cen,3,5),
    input(Cen,4,5),
    input(Cen,5,5),
    input(Cen,6,5),
    input(Cen,7,5),
    input(Cen,8,5),
    input(Cen,9,5).

% base reachable cells (original coloured cells)
reach(R0,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5,
    cell(R0,C).

% even colours – stretch towards the centre row
reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 < Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 > Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% odd colours – stretch away from the centre row
reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 >= 0,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% coloured cells in the output (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

output(R,C,Col) :-
    reach(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

output(R,C,5) :-
    input(R,C,5).

% predicate indicating a non‑zero colour appears in the cell
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a coloured value
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

% ------------------------------------------------------------
%  ARC‑AGI – stretch‑towards‑or‑away‑from‑the‑5‑wall
% ------------------------------------------------------------

% parity of colours (0..9 is hard‑coded)
even(C) :- color(C), C = 0.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 2.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 4.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 6.
even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% all board cells (0..9 × 0..9)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% centre row – the row that contains a 5 in every column
centre_row(Cen) :-
    input(Cen,0,5),
    input(Cen,1,5),
    input(Cen,2,5),
    input(Cen,3,5),
    input(Cen,4,5),
    input(Cen,5,5),
    input(Cen,6,5),
    input(Cen,7,5),
    input(Cen,8,5),
    input(Cen,9,5).

% base reachable cells (original coloured cells)
reach(R0,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5,
    cell(R0,C).

% even colours – stretch towards the centre row
reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 < Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 > Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% odd colours – stretch away from the centre row
reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 >= 0,
    not input(R1,C,5).

reach(R1,C,Col) :-
    reach(R0,C,Col),
    odd(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 + 1,
    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% coloured cells in the output (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

output(R,C,Col) :-
    reach(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

output(R,C,5) :-
    input(R,C,5).

% cells that finally have a non‑zero colour
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a coloured value
output(R,C,0) :- cell(R,C), not has_nonzero(R,C).

% at most one colour per cell
:- output(R,C,Col1), output(R,C,Col2), Col1 != Col2, cell(R,C).

#show output/3.

% parity of colours (0..9 is hard‑coded)

even(C) :- color(C), C = 8.

odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 1.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 3.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 5.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 7.
odd(C)  :- color(C), C = 9.

% all board cells (0..9 × 0..9)
cell(R,C) :- R = 0..9, C = 0..9.

% centre row – the row that contains a 5 in every column
centre_row(Cen) :-
    input(Cen,0,5),
    input(Cen,1,5),
    input(Cen,2,5),
    input(Cen,3,5),

    input(Cen,5,5),

    input(Cen,8,5),
    input(Cen,9,5).

% base reachable cells (original coloured cells)
reach(R0,C,Col) :-
    input(R0,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5,
    cell(R0,C).

% even colours – stretch towards the centre row

    reach(R0,C,Col),
    even(Col),

    cell(R0,C),

    not input(R1,C,5).

    even(Col),
    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 > Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 > Cen,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% odd colours – stretch away from the centre row

    centre_row(Cen),
    cell(R0,C),
    R0 < Cen,
    R1 = R0 - 1,
    R1 >= 0,
    not input(R1,C,5).

    R1 <= 9,
    not input(R1,C,5).

% coloured cells in the output (non‑zero, non‑5)
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,
    Col != 5.

output(R,C,Col) :-
    reach(R,C,Col),
    Col != 0,

output(R,C,5) :-

% cells that finally have a non‑zero colour
has_nonzero(R,C) :- output(R,C,Col), Col != 0.

% background colour 0 for cells without a coloured value

% at most one colour per cell
